title: Refactor Notifications to Derived State & Enhance Badges
description: Transition the badge and notification system from disjointed, standalone entities to a pure derived-state model. Badges will strictly reflect the real-time state of Chats, Agent Runs, and Git. Introduce robust user preferences for badge behaviors and custom colors.

features.title: Feature 1: Core Derived State & Visibility
features.description: Rip out notificationsService dependencies for badge counting. Calculate badge counts strictly from ChatsContext, GitContext, and AgentsContext. Badges show on non-selected projects in the sidebar, and top navigation items display badges for the currently selected project.

features.title: Feature 2: Git Badges & Metrics
features.description: Implement state derivation for Git repository status. Derive 'incoming commits' (pulls available) and 'uncommitted changes' (working tree dirty). Provide toggles to independently enable/disable badges for incoming vs. uncommitted changes. Default color: Green.

features.title: Feature 3: Chat & Agent Badges
features.description: Implement state derivation for Chats and Agent Runs. Add toggle for total unread vs. chats with unread. Runs show active + completed unread. Default colors: Chat (Red), Agent Runs (Blue).

features.title: Feature 4: Settings UI (Notification Preferences)
features.description: Update SettingsView.tsx to house the new preferences. Add toggles for Git (incoming vs. uncommitted) and Chat (total vs. chat count). Add a color picker for Chat, Git, and Agent Run badges. Ensure these settings are saved per-project or globally as defined by the user settings schema.

features.title: Final Feature: Git Unread State Consolidation & Clearance
features.description:
Resolve the stuck Git badge issue where `getProjectUpdatedBranchesCount` in `GitContext` returns a positive unread count that cannot be cleared because `GitView` provides no mechanism to mark branches as 'seen'.

**Root Cause:**
The Git badge is purely derived from `GitContext.unified.relToCurrent` (branches ahead of current). Unlike Chats (which have explicit read/unread tracking), Git has no 'last-seen SHA' concept in the renderer — so any branch that is ahead stays counted forever until it is merged or deleted.

**Work Items:**

1. **Audit `GitContext` unread model** — Confirm exactly which field drives `getProjectUpdatedBranchesCount`. Determine if there is already a `lastSeenSha` / `seenBranches` concept or if it needs to be introduced.

2. **Introduce a 'seen branches' store** — Add a lightweight per-project map `{ [branchName]: string /* headSha */ }` persisted via `appSettings` or a dedicated preferences key. When a user opens `GitView` for a project, mark all currently-listed branches as seen (store their current head SHA). A branch is only 'unread' if its head SHA has changed since it was last seen.

3. **Wire clearance on `GitView` mount/focus** — In `src/renderer/src/screens/GitView.tsx`, on mount and whenever the active project changes, call a new `markBranchesSeen(projectId)` action from `GitContext`. This action should update the seen-SHA store and trigger a recomputation of `getProjectUpdatedBranchesCount` so the badge drops to 0.

4. **Update `GitContext` badge derivation** — `getProjectUpdatedBranchesCount` must compare each branch's current head SHA against its last-seen SHA (not just whether `ahead > 0`). Only count a branch as unread if its SHA has advanced beyond what was last seen.

5. **Ensure badge re-arms correctly** — After clearing, if a branch later receives new commits (monitor event updates the SHA), it should reappear in the count. The seen-SHA store entry for that branch becomes stale and the count increments again.

**Files likely involved:**
- `src/renderer/src/contexts/GitContext.tsx`
- `src/renderer/src/screens/GitView.tsx`
- `src/renderer/src/hooks/useNotifications.ts` (or equivalent badge derivation hook)
- `src/renderer/src/navigation/SidebarView.tsx` (badge display)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Opening `GitView` clears the Git badge for the active project.
- The badge reappears if a branch subsequently receives new commits.
- No badge accumulation occurs on app restart for branches that were already seen.
- The fix is purely additive (no removal of existing Git monitoring or badge logic).
